product.title: Small Mug
product.body_html:
For our ever-growing Small Mug fan club, a petite vessel that makes every shelf cuter.

The Small Mug is a shortie version of the original and—truth be told—Team East Fork’s unofficial favorite. Just the right size for a cozy cup of tea, a traditional latte or fireside hot chocolate. No need to rush—this one keeps things warm without asking you to chug. Pair it with a Breakfast Bowl, Side Plate and Brass Spork for the sweetest little set (great for kids, but also just plain delightful). And when it sits next to The Mug and the Big Mug? The family feels complete, something just right for everyone who pulls up a seat at your table.

Why we love it:

  • Aeropress friendly
  • The cup for traditional cappuccino drinkers
  • Dishwasher and microwave safe 


  • Dimensions: 3.1” x 3.25”
  • Weight: .75 lb
  • Holds: 8 oz to the brim, 6 oz comfortably
  • Material: 100% Lead Free Stoneware
  • Our pottery is made by humans and reduction-fired in Asheville, North Carolina. Any differences in glaze applications and surfaces are there to embrace.

product.title: Crumbs
product.body_html:
Taste your way through desserts from more than 100 countries without leaving your kitchen. Organized geographically, each recipe begins with an origin story, followed by clear, step-by-step instructions and notes on how it’s made in different corners of the world.

Why we love it:
Features both old-world classics and intriguing local specialties
Perfect for bakers of all skill levels looking to expand their repertoire
The kind of cookbook you actually want to read, not just reference

product.title: Woven Grid Kitchen Towels, Set of 2
Pitcher in Taproot
product.body_html: Designed and made by The Long Confidence. Each pitcher is made by hand, using both a mold-making and finishing technique that yields a slightly different form each time. The work shows the mark of the hand and the evidence of its creation.

product.title: Tea Towel in Rosalba
product.body_html: This tea towel is a collaboration between Madda Studio and the weavers of El Camino de los Altos (ECLA). ECLA is an association of 130 Mayan women weavers from the highlands of Chiapas in Southern Mexico. This is the first product designed and produced in collaboration from beginning to end. The project provides an opportunity for newly trained weavers to put into practice the knowledge they have gained.

product.title: Patchwork Placemat in Grapefruit
product.body_html: This placemat features a linear pattern painted directly on the fabric in colors inspired by our Summer Collection. Each is painted by hand in ways that encourage subtle transition in organic ways adding soft visual texture to the sturdy linen fabric.

product.title: All Day Napkins in Moss Gingham (Set of 4)
product.body_html: These soft yet durable napkins add a handmade touch to your tablescape. Produced in small batches, each piece is unique, showcasing the exquisite craftsmanship of the Tikkiwallah weavers in Laos.

product.title: Origins No 5 Limited Print
product.body_html: A print drawing from Bienvenue Studios’ Origins risograph series—the inspiration for Heath’s 2026 summer collection. This Zurich-based studio’s signature biophilic design practice translates natural phenomena (flowers, stones, etc.) into intricate visual motifs. Origins is a study of seeds: tiny vessels containing infinite potential. Sold without frame.

product.title: Muir Flatware in Amber (5 Piece Setting)
product.body_html: Simple, satisfying to hold, and usable every day, for any occasion — just like our dinnerware. The first flatware pattern, start to finish, made in the US in almost a decade. Design-led in San Francisco by Heath, and hand-tooled and crafted in New York by Sherrill Manufacturing. Features a golden matte finish in Amber.

product.title: Medium Candle Caddy in Glacier
product.body_html: An eye-catching centerpiece for your table, this hand-thrown caddy serves as both a platform for your favorite sauces, dips, and seasonings, as well as an elegant holder for a taper candle. Available for a limited time in Glacier as part of our winter collection.

product.title: Tela Placemat in Cameo Pink (Set of 4)
product.body_html: The Tela (Italian for "canvas") fabric is very soft yet strong and tactile, perfect for an informal yet elegant table setting. After hand sewing each item goes through a special dying and stone washing process to create the soft and weathered look. The result is a very unique effect with variation which makes each piece unique.

product.title: Tinto Filo Napkin in Isabella Beige (Set of 2)
product.body_html: Each napkin is hand-embroidered and made from beautiful, yarn-dyed linen. The fabric is woven on vintage shuttle looms by a duo of craftswomen in Italy. The warp and weft threads are dyed slightly different colors resulting in a visually textured quality to the fabric.

product.title: Hopsack Cushion in Isabella Beige
product.body_html: Each cushion is made from beautiful, yarn-dyed linen with a luxurious hand. The fabric is woven on vintage shuttle looms by a duo of craftswomen in Italy. The warp and weft threads are dyed slightly different colors resulting in a visually textured quality to the fabric and different colored fringe on opposite edges.

product.title: Hopsack Cushion in Moro Brown

product.title: Veda Pillow in Rust
product.body_html: The designs on these textiles are inspired by the travel diaries of Soil to Studio founder Swati Bansal, covering journeys through remote villages and towns across Europe, England, and the Middle East. Highly skilled craftspeople—who have been trained in the art of block printing for generations—make each pillow by hand in Jaipur, India. The print featured here perfectly complements the Summer Collection.

product.title: Desert Market Seeds in Purple Prickly Pear
product.body_html: Opuntia violacea v. Santa Rita—or the purple prickly pear—is a plum-colored cactus with bright yellow flowers. As a desert plant, it favors drier soil, lots of sunlight, and minimal watering. Germination takes 2–3 weeks, and flowers emerge after 2–3 years.

product.title: Desert Market Seeds in California Poppy
product.body_html: Eschsholtzia californica—better known as California poppy—is an orange, cup-shaped flower, ranging from 2–4” in diameter. For best results, plant in light to sandy soil with plenty of sunshine. Germination takes 15–30 days. Blooming period: April–August.

product.title: “Trillium No. 2” Tetra Pak Print
product.body_html:
Today we share with you a new kind of adventure. I have been – for the last 97 days – learning the technique of dry point printing through observing the world around me, focusing on the day-to-day life of a flower, a goose couple, and of course, Pearl. Each year I set out on an adventure to learn a new process which helps me share Peg and Awl treasures whilst simultaneously offering me ever-desired new ways of seeing and storytelling. 

This collection – Little Windows – is made of trash and scrap from engraving to framing, and each little work is unique. I am hand-printing editions of 10 of each piece.

PS: In addition to using trash as my sub straight, I’ve printed on scrap paper from journals we make at Peg and Awl. Walter made frames out of scrap FSC certified oak from furniture and other treasures we make here. I used the rest of the wood scrap to heat up our sauna on Temperature Terrace, stop 6 on the map. The framer, too, rejoiced in using their scrap museum glass and board for the framing.

Tetra Pak Printing

Tetra Pak is a Swedish company that specializes in their iconic and sustainable paper-based cartons, used for milks, broths, and my favourite ? rose water!

It is also a magical carton, in which, after enjoying and washing the contents, can be cut apart and used as a plate for print making using the dry point method.

With a sharp tool, engraving into the silver side of the plate is relatively simple. After engraving, the ink is spread onto the plate then wiped off, so ink remains in the engraved lines and wherever else darkness is desired. After wetting and blotting the paper, a sandwich of felt, scrap paper, inked plate, wet paper, top felt, is made ready for the press. After rolling the sandwich through the press, the etching comes to life! Each of these prints was made in this way with much trial and error and discovery! The frames were made in from scrap FSC certified Oak, scrap museum glass and matt board, making this a solidly ecologically sound treasure.

This collection – Little Windows – is made of trash and scrap from engraving to framing, and each little work is unique. I am hand-printing editions of 10 of each piece.

PS: In addition to using trash as my sub straight, I’ve printed on scrap paper from journals we make at Peg and Awl. Walter made frames out of scrap FSC certified oak from furniture and other treasures we make here. I used the rest of the wood scrap to heat up our sauna on Temperature Terrace, stop 6 on the map. The framer, too, rejoiced in using their scrap museum glass and board for the framing.

Tetra Pak Printing

Tetra Pak is a Swedish company that specializes in their iconic and sustainable paper-based cartons, used for milks, broths, and my favourite ? rose water!

It is also a magical carton, in which, after enjoying and washing the contents, can be cut apart and used as a plate for print making using the dry point method.

With a sharp tool, engraving into the silver side of the plate is relatively simple. After engraving, the ink is spread onto the plate then wiped off, so ink remains in the engraved lines and wherever else darkness is desired. After wetting and blotting the paper, a sandwich of felt, scrap paper, inked plate, wet paper, top felt, is made ready for the press. After rolling the sandwich through the press, the etching comes to life! Each of these prints was made in this way with much trial and error and discovery! The frames were made in from scrap FSC certified Oak, scrap museum glass and matt board, making this a solidly ecologically sound treasure.

Walter made frames out of scrap FSC certified oak from furniture and other treasures we make here. I used the rest of the wood scrap to heat up our sauna on Temperature Terrace, stop 6 on the map. The framer, too, rejoiced in using their scrap museum glass and board for the framing.

Tetra Pak Printing

Tetra Pak is a Swedish company that specializes in their iconic and sustainable paper-based cartons, used for milks, broths, and my favourite ? rose water!

It is also a magical carton, in which, after enjoying and washing the contents, can be cut apart and used as a plate for print making using the dry point method.

With a sharp tool, engraving into the silver side of the plate is relatively simple. After engraving, the ink is spread onto the plate then wiped off, so ink remains in the engraved lines and wherever else darkness is desired. After wetting and blotting the paper, a sandwich of felt, scrap paper, inked plate, wet paper, top felt, is made ready for the press. After rolling the sandwich through the press, the etching comes to life! Each of these prints was made in this way with much trial and error and discovery! The frames were made in from scrap FSC certified Oak, scrap museum glass and matt board, making this a solidly ecologically sound treasure.
